metadata.title: EM Consulting — College Admissions Strategy
metadata.description: Wharton Huntsman Dual Degree and MIT admit. Structured admissions consulting for Chinese families applying to U.S. universities.

hero.title: One application. One systematic plan.
hero.subtitle: Huntsman Program · MIT and Wharton dual admit. A structured five-milestone plan for the full U.S. application.
hero.ctaSubtext: A 90-minute session to assess your child's competitiveness and talk through next steps.

items.title: MIT + Wharton, same cycle
items.description: Decade-old admissions experience is the industry standard. We took a different route: we just met these standards ourselves. The signals that matter now are the signals we just read.

items.title: Five milestones, real deliverables
items.description: Every stage produces a written document and an acceptance checkpoint: positioning deck, profile roadmap, finalized essays, submission checklist, decision framework. This is a trackable strategy engagement, not open-ended coaching.

items.title: Selective by design
items.description: We turn down students we can't actually help, and we take cases where we expect a good result. That's fair to your family and to us.

items.title: Native bilingual communication
items.description: We speak Mandarin natively with parents and English natively with students, so nothing gets lost in translation or in cultural context.

items.title: Founding cohort · max 10
items.description: The free 90-minute diagnostic gives a candid assessment of your child's competitiveness. We accept up to 10 families this application cycle, and the assessment is yours to keep whether we work together or not.

process.title: Five milestones with written deliverables at every stage

milestones.title: Strategic positioning
milestones.deliverable: Delivered: positioning deck with core narrative direction, initial school list (8–12), and essay topic map.

milestones.title: Profile optimization
milestones.deliverable: Delivered: profile roadmap covering activity adjustments, recommendation strategy, and test timeline.

milestones.title: Essay development
milestones.deliverable: Delivered: finalized essays with 3–4 feedback rounds each. All content written by the student.

milestones.title: Application review
milestones.deliverable: Delivered: submission checklist with full materials review, mock interviews, and a written parent brief.

milestones.title: Post-submission
milestones.deliverable: Delivered: decision analysis framework covering waitlist strategy and multi-offer comparison.

trackRecord.title: Founder credentials and prior mentorship
trackRecord.subtitle: EM Consulting was founded in 2026. Below are the founders' own admissions results, plus the schools where students Eric mentored (before starting the firm) were admitted.

founderCreds.name: Mary Liu
founderCreds.detail: Same cycle: MIT (EA, Astrophysics) and Wharton (ED)

trackRecord.mentorshipLabel: Schools where Eric's mentees were admitted (pre-firm)

testimonial.quote: Eric helped me through the entire process — narrowing my school list, sharpening my extracurriculars, and figuring out my personal statement. I was stuck on what story to tell, and he gave me a framework that made it click. He also reviewed all my supplementals and helped me apply to scholarships I wouldn't have found on my own. It made the whole thing a lot less stressful.
testimonial.attribution: Duke '29 · mentored by Eric before EM Consulting

trackRecord.antiObjection: Our track record is our own admissions. We met the same standards your child now faces.

pricing.title: Transparent pricing
pricing.note: Every engagement begins with a free 90-minute diagnostic; no contract and no fee required. After confirming fit, pricing depends on school count (8–12) and service depth.

audit.name: Admissions Diagnostic Report
audit.description: Standalone written report after a 90-minute session: positioning, competitiveness analysis, strategic recommendations. Purchasable on its own, no obligation to continue.

full.name: Full Package
full.description: Five-milestone engagement covering 8–12 schools. All written deliverables included. Parent updates at every milestone, with additional check-ins during intensive phases.

items.a: Decade-old admissions experience is the industry standard, and we took a different route: we're the applicants who just met these standards. Former AOs can spot adult-ghostwritten essays; our age makes it easier for students to write in their own voice. Top swimming coaches usually aren't Olympic champions. They notice what the athlete can't see about their own form.

Admissions decisions are made by the schools, entirely beyond our control. We operate on the McKinsey model: fees compensate for time, expertise, and deliverables, not for outcome. Agencies that promise admission usually promise schools your child could get into without them. Our work is on the schools that require real strategy.

items.q: What if you decline us after the diagnostic?
items.a: The 90-minute diagnostic is free regardless. If we determine fit isn't right, you still leave with a candid assessment of your child's competitiveness. If you want that assessment in writing, the standalone Admissions Audit ($300–500) covers the same ground as a formal written report.

items.q: How many hours per week does the student commit?
items.a: It varies by milestone. Month 1 (Positioning): 2–3 hours/week. Months 2–4 (Profile): 1–2 hours/week plus activity time. Months 4–7 (Essay peak): 5–8 hours/week across multiple drafts. Months 7–8 (Review): 3–5 hours/week. Months 8–10 (Post-submission): 1–2 hours/week. Most of that time is the student's own writing and revision.

items.q: How are you different from legacy agencies like New Oriental or Crimson?
items.a: We don't position ourselves by comparison to the rest of the market. Our shape is simpler: two founders who just admitted to MIT and Wharton, native Mandarin with parents, five milestones with written deliverables at each stage. That's what we do.

items.q: How do you prevent ghostwriting concerns on essays?
items.a: We never write or substantially rewrite any application material. We brainstorm topics, suggest outlines, and give feedback on drafts the student writes. Each essay runs 3–4 rounds, with written records kept for every revision. This policy is codified in our service agreement, and any violation ends the engagement without refund.

items.q: What is the refund and cancellation policy?
items.a: Full terms are in our service agreement. Summary: Before Milestone 1 (Positioning Deck) delivery: first installment (50%) refunded, minus a $500 administrative fee. After Milestone 1, before Milestone 3 (Essay phase): first installment non-refundable (core deliverables received); second installment not yet collected. After Milestone 3 commences: both installments non-refundable (most labor-intensive phase). Either party may terminate with 30 days written notice; pro-rata refund applies to milestones not yet commenced. The free diagnostic carries no fee and requires no contract.
